issues.description: VAL-DB-003 throughput test shows 6.6% degradation instead of expected 5% improvement. The double-buffer mechanism adds overhead (memcpy_d2d_async, buffer swap) that isn't compensated by compute-communication overlap in current workload.
issues.suggestedFix: Consider profiling stream event timing to identify optimization opportunities. The original VAL-DB-003 assertion may be overly optimistic for this workload.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Double-buffer mode requires both cached_dispatch and stream_overlap_dispatch to be enabled. The mechanism only activates when use_double_buffer=True in _decode_step_cached_stream.
sharedStateObservations.description: Document double-buffer mode requirements and dispatch mode interactions

sharedStateObservations.observation: memcpy_d2d_async method added to HIPRuntime and GPUDevice classes. Required for copying d_hidden to d_hidden_write before residual add in double-buffer mode.
sharedStateObservations.description: New API method for async device-to-device memory copy

summary: Fixed VAL-DB-002 numerical correctness by updating cached LaunchSpec pointers at runtime. Root cause was stale d_hidden pointers in pre-built LaunchSpecs. Fix verified on dev server with min cosine similarity 0.999949. Implementation is correct and follows project patterns.
evidenceReviewed: git show d387173 - code changes to tp_engine.py
